county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Adair County formed 1851 from Cass. County seat is Greenfield.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Greenfield holds deeds from 1855; Clerk of District Court has probate and court records. Adair County was settled primarily by American-born migrants from Ohio, Indiana, and Illinois in the 1850s. Check the Adair County Historical Society in Greenfield for family files and county histories. The Nodaway River valley settlers left church records in Methodist and Christian Church (Disciples) congregations. FamilySearch has microfilmed Adair County land and probate records. BLM GLO land patents are online for original federal land entries. Adair County Free Press (est. 1873) has obituaries and legal notices.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Adair County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Greenfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Greenfield
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Greenfield / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Adams County formed 1851 from Pottawattamie. County seat is Corning.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Corning holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Adams County was carved from Pottawattamie in 1851 and attracted settlers from the upper South and Midwest. The Adams County Free Press and Corning newspapers are valuable for obituaries. Check the Adams County Genealogical Society for surname files and cemetery transcriptions. Icarian Colony connections -- the French Icarian utopian community at Corning (1852-1898) left unique communal records now at the Iowa State Historical Society. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch has county microfilm.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Adams County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Allamakee County formed 1847 from Clayton. County seat is Waukon.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Waukon holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate and court records. Allamakee County in the northeastern corner was settled by New Englanders, Norwegians, and Germans. Norwegian Lutheran church records (Decorah area nearby) are essential. Effigy Mounds National Monument is in the county. Check the Allamakee County Historical Society and Lansing Historical Museum. The county borders the Mississippi River with early steamboat landing communities at Lansing and New Albin. BLM GLO patents online. Yellow River State Forest area settlers documented in early land records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Allamakee County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Appanoose County formed 1843 from Davis. County seat is Centerville.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Centerville holds deeds from 1846; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Appanoose County was a significant coal mining region in south-central Iowa. Coal company records, mine accident reports, and mining community newspapers document immigrant miners (Welsh, English, Swedish, Italian). Centerville newspapers from the 1860s have extensive obituaries. The Appanoose County Historical Society maintains family files. Strong Quaker presence -- Unionville and Cincinnati Friends Meeting records. Many settlers came from Missouri and southern states. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Appanoose County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Audubon County formed 1851 from Cass. County seat is Audubon.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Audubon holds deeds from 1855; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Audubon County was settled predominantly by Danish and German immigrants in the 1870s-1880s. Elk Horn and Kimballton are the largest Danish settlements in the United States -- Danish Immigrant Museum (now Museum of Danish America) in Elk Horn has family records, church registers, and immigrant letters. Danish Lutheran church records are essential. Audubon County Historical Society in Exira. BLM GLO patents online. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Audubon County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Benton County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Vinton.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Vinton holds deeds from 1846; Clerk of District Court has probate and court records. Benton County was among the first organized after the 1837 Black Hawk Purchase. Bohemian (Czech) settlers arrived in the 1850s-1860s, making this area one of the earliest Czech settlements in Iowa. ZCBJ (Czech fraternal) lodge records and Czech Catholic/Brethren church registers are important. Iowa Braille School in Vinton (est. 1853) has institutional records. Benton County Historical Society in Vinton. FamilySearch microfilm for land and probate. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Benton County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Black Hawk County formed 1843 from Delaware. County seat is Waterloo.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Waterloo holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Black Hawk County is a major urban county centered on Waterloo-Cedar Falls. The Rath Packing Company and John Deere Waterloo Works employment records document working families. Significant African American community arrived for meatpacking jobs from the 1910s-1940s -- Waterloo's East Side history. Grout Museum of History and Science has local collections. University of Northern Iowa library has regional materials. German Lutheran and Catholic church records are strong. FamilySearch microfilm. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Black Hawk County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Boone County formed 1846 from Polk. County seat is Boone.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Boone holds deeds from 1849; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Boone County was an important coal mining area and railroad center. The Chicago & North Western Railway and other lines made Boone a division point -- railroad employment records at Boone County Historical Society. Kate Shelley Railroad Museum preserves railroad heritage. Swedish settlers in the Boxholm-Ogden area left Lutheran church records. Ledges State Park area was early settlement. Iowa State University (Ames, in Story County) holds regional agricultural records. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Boone County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Bremer County formed 1851 from Winnebago (old) / Indian lands. County seat is Waverly.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Waverly holds deeds from 1853; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Bremer County was settled heavily by German immigrants, particularly from Mecklenburg and Hannover. German Lutheran church records (Missouri Synod and Iowa Synod) are essential. Wartburg College in Waverly (German Lutheran, est. 1852) has institutional and regional church archives. Bremer County Historical Society in Waverly. Plainfield and Tripoli communities have strong German heritage records. FamilySearch microfilm available. BLM GLO patents online. The Cedar River valley attracted early settlers.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Bremer County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.

source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Waverly
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books, mortgages, and land records from 1851 to present. County Recorder maintains grantor/grantee indexes.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records substantially complete from county organization

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County Probate Records -- Clerk of District Court
source_profiles.repository: Clerk of District Court, Waverly
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, estates, guardianships, and probate proceedings from 1851. Clerk of District Court handles all probate matters in Iowa.

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County Marriage Records
source_profiles.repository: County Recorder, Waverly / Iowa Department of Public Health
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and certificates from 1851. County-level records; state registration began 1880. FamilySearch has indexed many Iowa marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents (homesteads, cash sales, military warrants) for Bremer County. Iowa is a public land state; most land was transferred from federal ownership between 1838 and 1870.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records complete; originals at NARA

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Bremer County. Iowa territorial census 1836, 1838; Iowa state census 1847, 1849, 1851-1925 (every 2 years). Agricultural schedules available.

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County Cemetery Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa Genealogical Society / Find A Grave / Bremer County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Bremer County. Township cemeteries, church cemeteries, and city cemeteries. Iowa Genealogical Society and county historical societies have published transcription volumes. Find A Grave has extensive Iowa coverage.

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County Tax Lists and Assessment Records
source_profiles.repository: Iowa State Archives, Des Moines / Bremer County Auditor, Waverly
source_profiles.coverage_notes: County tax lists, personal property assessments, and plat books for Bremer County. Tax lists name property owners and describe holdings. Iowa State Archives holds some transferred tax records on microfilm.

source_profiles.source_name: Bremer County German Lutheran and Catholic Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Various -- diocesan archives, synodical archives, FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Lutheran and Catholic church records for Bremer County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Buchanan County formed 1837 from Dubuque (Indian Cession). County seat is Independence.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Independence holds deeds from 1847; Clerk of District Court has probate records. Buchanan County was settled by New Englanders and German immigrants in the 1840s-1850s. Independence State Mental Health Institute (est. 1873) institutional records may document patients. Buchanan County Historical Society and Independence Heritage Museum. Wapsipinicon River communities. Strong Methodist and Presbyterian church records from early settlement. Jesup and Quasqueton communities. FamilySearch microfilm available. BLM GLO patents online.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Iowa Land Records (iowalandrecords.org) provides online access to Buchanan County land records. Coverage varies by county; many have records from the mid-1800s to present. BLM GLO patents available at glorecords.blm.gov for original federal entries.
